answer:
The Berry–Keating conjecture relates the classical Hamiltonian \( H = xp \) to the nontrivial zeros of the Riemann zeta function, suggesting that its quantization yields eigenvalues corresponding to the imaginary parts of those zeros. The most common answer is that the spectrum is similar to the **nontrivial zeros of the Riemann zeta function**.  

\boxed{\text{nontrivial zeros of the Riemann zeta function}}
